name: aromatic nitro
note: Aromatic nitro groups are reduced to reactive nitroso / hydroxylamine intermediates (bioactivation); associated with idiosyncratic hepato- and haematotoxicity.
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Curr Drug Metab 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: aromatic amine (aniline)
note: Free aromatic (anilino) amines undergo CYP/peroxidase N-oxidation to nitroso / nitrenium species that bind protein and DNA; classic bioactivation liability.
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Curr Drug Metab 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: anilide / arylamide
note: Acetanilide-type amides can be hydrolysed to the free aniline and then bioactivated (e.g. acetaminophen -> NAPQI pathway class).
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Chem Res Toxicol 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: thiophene
note: Thiophene rings are oxidised to S-oxides / reactive epoxide-like intermediates (bioactivation); implicated in tienilic-acid / ticlopidine hepatotoxicity.
citation: Stepan et al., Chem Res Toxicol 2011 (PMID 21661688)

name: furan
note: Furan rings are epoxidised by CYP to reactive cis-enedial / ene-dione species that adduct protein; established hepatotoxic bioactivation motif.
citation: Stepan et al., Chem Res Toxicol 2011 (PMID 21661688)

name: hydrazine / hydrazide
note: Hydrazine and acyl-hydrazide moieties are oxidised to diazene / radical species and reactive acyl intermediates; isoniazid-type hepatotoxic liability.
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Curr Drug Metab 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: thiourea
note: Thiourea / thioamide sulfur is oxidised to reactive sulfenic/sulfinic acids and can deplete glutathione; thyroid and hepatic toxicity.
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Curr Drug Metab 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: epoxide
note: Epoxides are intrinsically electrophilic and alkylate protein/DNA nucleophiles directly; a reactive-metabolite hard alert.
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Curr Drug Metab 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: Michael-acceptor enone
note: alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyls are soft electrophiles that undergo Michael addition with thiol nucleophiles (glutathione / protein cysteine).
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Curr Drug Metab 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: quinone
note: Quinones are redox-cycling electrophiles (ROS generation + Michael acceptors); a frequent bioactivation endpoint of phenols/aminophenols.
citation: Bolton et al., Chem Res Toxicol 2000; Kalgutkar 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: para-aminophenol (quinone-imine former)
note: para-Aminophenols are oxidised to reactive quinone-imines (the acetaminophen/NAPQI archetype); direct protein arylation.
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Curr Drug Metab 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: alpha-halo carbonyl
note: alpha-Halo ketones/amides are direct-acting alkylating electrophiles toward protein thiols.
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Curr Drug Metab 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: terminal aryl alkyne
note: Terminal aryl acetylenes are oxidised by CYP to ketene / oxirene intermediates that acylate the heme or apoprotein (mechanism-based inactivation).
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Curr Drug Metab 2005 (PMID 15720203)

name: free thiol
note: Free thiols autoxidise to reactive disulfides / sulfenic acids and can drive redox stress; captopril-type immunogenic hapten liability.
citation: Kalgutkar et al., Curr Drug Metab 2005 (PMID 15720203)
